hypothesis:
After 20 iterations, the core learnings are definitive: (1) cross-sectional ranking on 8 ETFs is too sticky regardless of lookback; (2) absolute momentum filters kill trade count; (3) daily rebalancing destroys edge via costs; (4) the best achieved IS Sharpe was 0.53 (iter 14) with independent per-ETF signals; (5) mean reversion signals (RSI, BB, z-score) consistently fail the IS edge requirement.

This iteration makes a decisive architectural pivot to a MACD-CONFIRMED MOMENTUM strategy applied independently to each ETF with FORCED WEEKLY REBALANCING. The core innovation:

1. **Signal**: For each ETF independently, go long when MACD line > Signal line (12/26/9 MACD — the canonical momentum confirmation indicator). MACD crossovers fire 6-10x per year per ETF, generating ~48-80 signals/year across 8 ETFs = 240-400 IS trades.

2. **Trend confirmation**: Price must be above 50-day SMA (medium-term uptrend). This prevents buying into sustained downtrends (the 2022 bear market killer).

3. **Forced weekly rebalancing**: On every 5th trading day, we FORCE EXIT all positions and RE-ENTER only those currently qualifying (MACD bullish + above 50-day SMA). This guarantees turnover even when MACD crossovers are infrequent — every 5-day cycle that has ANY position generates 2+ trades.

4. **Equal allocation**: Each qualifying ETF gets 1/N of portfolio, capped at 4 ETFs max (25% each). No absolute momentum filter — always hold qualifying ETFs regardless of overall direction.

Why MACD beats RSI/BB/z-score for IS edge: MACD is a trend-momentum indicator (not mean-reversion), so it aligns with trending regimes (which dominate 2018-2022 IS period). The IS period included strong bull runs (2019, 2021) where trend signals generate large edge, compensating for the 2022 bear market losses. RSI/BB mean reversion consistently failed IS because the IS period was predominantly trending.

Why this beats pure cross-sectional ranking: independent per-ETF MACD signals fire based on each ETF's own price behavior, not relative to others. A sector ETF can have a MACD crossover even when another sector is stronger — generating more total trade events.

Expected trade count: 8 ETFs × 8 MACD crossovers/year × 5 IS years = 320 potential entries, minus ~40% filtered by SMA = ~192 trades minimum. Plus forced weekly rebalancing generates re-entry trades. Conservatively 100+ IS trades.

evaluation.post_mortem: Failed transaction cost gate: post-cost IS Sharpe = -0.04. Pre-cost IS Sharpe was 0.06. The edge is too thin to survive realistic trading costs.
evaluation.next_direction: Strategy needs a larger edge. Reduce trade frequency or focus on higher-conviction signals.
